description_long: This is a full-sized axe featuring a long, slightly curved handle made of wood with a dark grip section at the end, likely for improved handling. The head of the axe is robust and painted a vivid red, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping wood. The blade edge is straight and wide, designed for efficient cutting, while the poll is squared off for striking. The handle transitions from a natural wood color to a dark, possibly rubberized grip, enhancing control and comfort during use. The mounting of the blade appears traditional, with the head fitted securely onto the handle. No visible markings or labels are present on the axe, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A full-sized axe with a red, wedge-shaped blade and a long, slightly curved wooden handle with a dark grip. The design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the axe, highlighting the red blade and the transition from wood to dark grip on the handle.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the width of the blade and the handle's curvature, with the blade edge facing forward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the flat poll of the axe head and the handle's length and color transition.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the blade and the handle's curve, with the poll visible.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved wooden handle with a dark, likely rubberized grip; oval cross-section for ergonomic hold.
attribute_blade_design: Single, broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge and a squared poll; painted red for visibility.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
